sections.paragraph:
-

(1) i.q. Arab. قاس to be bent as a bow, a circle, the back, II. to bend as a bow, comp. Gr. γαῦσος, bent. Hence קֶשֶׁת, قَوْسُ a bow, and pr.n. קִישֹׁון.

(2) i.q. יָקשׁ to lay snares. Once in fut. Isa 29:21, יְקשׁוּן or, as other copies read יְקֹושׁוּן.

Derivatives, see No. 1, also pr.n. קִישׁ, אֶלְקֹושׁי, and-
